prompt:
Remove the user profile form from the settings modal. The profile data (name, goal, age, days_per_week, time_available, injury_history, equipment) is write-only — it's saved to localStorage when edited and re-loaded into the form when the modal opens, and included in the export JSON, but nothing else in the app reads it. So the form collects data that has no functional effect.

The user wants the settings modal to contain only:
1. Dark/light toggle (already there)
2. Import / Export buttons (already there)
3. Version display at the bottom (already there)

…and the profile section removed entirely.

## Scope of changes

Push commits to this PR's existing branch (agent/20260426-0850-0430).

### index.html
Remove the entire .profile-section block in the settings modal (the parent <div class='profile-section'> containing 'Profile' label and all 7 .profile-row entries: name, goal, age, days_per_week, time_available, injury_history, equipment).

### app.js
Remove all profile-related code:
- The PROFILE_KEY constant
- loadProfile(), saveProfile(), saveProfileField() functions
- renderProfileEditor() function
- The '.profile-field' change-event listener (around line 532)
- The migration block that calls loadProfile() and saveProfile() on first load (around lines 67-84) — replace with a one-time localStorage cleanup that removes the 'basement_lab_profile' key if it exists, so users with old data don't accumulate cruft. Or just leave the orphan key — your call; cleanup is nicer but not essential.
- In the export-sanitization block (around line 882-914), remove profile reading and the profile sanitization fields (name, goal, age, time_available, days_per_week, injury_history, equipment). The export should still include workout-history data; just drop profile.

Also: any callers of renderProfileEditor() (e.g. inside the settings modal open handler) — remove those calls.

### tests/e2e.spec.js
Remove tests that exercise the profile form:
- Anything checking #profile-name, #profile-goal, #profile-age, #profile-equipment visibility (around lines 640-660ish)
- Anything that types into profile fields or asserts profile data round-trips
- Anything in the import/export tests that asserts profile fields appear in the exported JSON — adjust to match the new export shape (workout history only)

Don't remove tests for: settings modal open/close, dark/light toggle, import button, export button, version display.

### style.css
Remove rules that exclusively target profile UI:
- .profile-section
- .profile-section-label
- .profile-fields
- .profile-row
- .profile-row-pair
- .profile-field

Don't remove generic rules that the profile UI happened to use (e.g. shared input styling); those still apply to other inputs.

## Acceptance

- All Playwright tests pass: 'nix develop -c npm test' (or whatever the project uses).
- Open the preview/local serve, open the settings modal, confirm only dark/light, import, export, version are shown — no profile fields.
- Export JSON should still produce valid output with workout history; just no profile section.
- Don't touch other parts of the design refresh — this is purely a removal.

## Commit message

Use a concise commit on the existing PR branch:

  refactor: drop unused user profile form from settings

  The profile fields (name, goal, age, days_per_week, time_available,
  injury_history, equipment) only round-tripped localStorage and the
  export JSON — nothing read them to drive app behavior. Remove the
  form, related JS, CSS, and tests. Cleanup of the
  basement_lab_profile localStorage key for existing users.

No new PR — append to the existing PR #61. The reviewer will see this as another commit in the design-refresh review.
